raw.criterion: <fact>The study uses the UCI heart disease dataset.</fact><scope>Scope is the source and identity of the dataset used in the experiments.</scope>
<fact>The UCI heart disease dataset used in this study includes 920 patient records.</fact><scope>Scope is the size of the dataset instance employed in the experiments.</scope>
<fact>The 920 patient records in the dataset come from four hospitals.</fact><scope>Scope is the institutional distribution of the data sources.</scope>
<fact>The hospitals providing the patient records are located in the USA, Hungary, and Switzerland.</fact><scope>Scope is the geographical distribution of the data sources.</scope>
<fact>The dataset is named the Long Beach VA dataset.</fact><scope>Long Beach VA dataset</scope>
<fact>The Long Beach VA dataset consists of patients who underwent cardiac catheterization.</fact><scope>Long Beach VA dataset patient inclusion criteria</scope>

<fact>The cardiac catheterizations in the Long Beach VA dataset were performed at the Long Beach Veterans Administration Medical Center.</fact><scope>Long Beach VA dataset patient inclusion criteria</scope>
<fact>The cardiac catheterizations in the Long Beach VA dataset were performed between 1984 and 1987.</fact><scope>Long Beach VA dataset temporal scope</scope>

<fact>The Long Beach VA dataset includes all individuals who underwent cardiac catheterization at the Long Beach Veterans Administration Medical Center between 1984 and 1987.</fact><scope>Long Beach VA dataset inclusion completeness</scope>
<fact>Patients with prior infarction were excluded from the dataset.</fact><scope>Full patient cohort prior to any exclusions.</scope>
<fact>Patients with valvular disease were excluded from the dataset.</fact><scope>Full patient cohort prior to any exclusions.</scope>
<fact>Patients with prior catheterization were excluded from the dataset.</fact><scope>Full patient cohort prior to any exclusions.</scope>

<fact>After applying all specified exclusions, there were 200 patient data points remaining in the dataset.</fact><scope>Dataset after exclusion of patients with prior infarction, valvular disease, and prior catheterization.</scope>
<fact>The Hungary dataset is comprised of patients.</fact><scope>Hungary dataset; patient composition</scope>
<fact>The patients in the Hungary dataset underwent catheterization.</fact><scope>Hungary dataset; patient procedures</scope>
<fact>The patients in the Hungary dataset were treated at the Hungarian Institute of Cardiology.</fact><scope>Hungary dataset; healthcare facility</scope>
<fact>The Hungarian Institute of Cardiology is located in Budapest.</fact><scope>Hungarian Institute of Cardiology; location</scope>
<fact>The patients in the Hungary dataset underwent catheterization between 1983 and 1987 inclusive.</fact><scope>Hungary dataset; data collection time range</scope>
<fact>The Switzerland dataset is a distinct dataset.</fact><scope>Switzerland dataset definition</scope>
<fact>The Switzerland dataset uses data collected from individuals.</fact><scope>Switzerland dataset population source</scope>
<fact>The individuals in the Switzerland dataset underwent cardiac catheterization.</fact><scope>Switzerland dataset population clinical procedure</scope>
<fact>The individuals in the Switzerland dataset were treated at university hospitals.</fact><scope>Switzerland dataset clinical setting</scope>
<fact>The Switzerland dataset includes individuals from the university hospital in Zurich, Switzerland.</fact><scope>Switzerland dataset geographic and institutional source</scope>
<fact>The Switzerland dataset includes individuals from the university hospital in Basel, Switzerland.</fact><scope>Switzerland dataset geographic and institutional source</scope>
<fact>The data for the Switzerland dataset was collected in 1985.</fact><scope>Switzerland dataset data collection time frame</scope>
<fact>The Switzerland dataset excludes patients similar to those excluded in the Long Beach VA dataset.</fact><scope>Switzerland dataset exclusion criteria</scope>
<fact>There are 76 total attributes in the dataset.</fact><scope>Global description of the dataset's schema.</scope>
<fact>All research conducted on the dataset used only the 14 features indicated in Table I.</fact><scope>Usage of dataset attributes in prior research.</scope>
<fact>Table I lists 14 features.</fact><scope>Structure of Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 3 is named 'age'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'age' is described as 'age in years'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'age' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 4 is named 'sex'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'sex' uses the encoding 1 = male.</fact><scope>Value encoding for the 'sex' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'sex' uses the encoding 0 = female.</fact><scope>Value encoding for the 'sex' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 9 is named 'cp'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'cp' is described as 'chest pain type'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'cp' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 10 is named 'trestbps'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'trestbps' is described as 'resting blood pressure'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'trestbps' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 12 is named 'chol'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'chol' is described as 'serum cholesterol'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'chol' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 16 is named 'fbs'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'fbs' is described as 'fasting blood sugar'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'fbs' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 19 is named 'restecg'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'restecg' is described as 'resting ECG results'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'restecg' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 32 is named 'thalach'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'thalach' is described as 'max heart rate'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'thalach' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 38 is named 'exang'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'exang' is described as 'exercise induced angina'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'exang' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 40 is named 'oldpeak'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'oldpeak' is described as 'ST depression due to exercise'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'oldpeak' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 41 is named 'slope'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'slope' is described as 'slope of exercise ST segment'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'slope' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 44 is named 'ca'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'ca' is described as '# of major vessels'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'ca' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 51 is named 'thal'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'thal' is described as 'Thalassemia disorder presence'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'thal' feature.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature with Feature Number 58 is named 'num'.</fact><scope>Mapping between feature numbers and names in Table I.</scope>
<fact>In Table I, the feature 'num' is described as 'the predicted attribute'.</fact><scope>Semantic description of the 'num' feature.</scope>

<fact>For a fair comparison, we follow the data preprocessing steps from [7], including removing entries with missing values (reducing the dataset to 740 points), using a $6 6 \% - 3 4 \%$ train-test split, converting labels $\mathbf { \dot { \theta } } 1 - 4 \mathbf { \overrightarrow { \theta } }$ to ”1” for binary classification (0 indicating no heart disease and 1 for heart disease), and normalizing the dataset.</fact> <scope>uci</scope>
raw.fact: For a fair comparison, we follow the data preprocessing steps from [7], including removing entries with missing values (reducing the dataset to 740 points), using a $6 6 \% - 3 4 \%$ train-test split, converting labels $\mathbf { \dot { \theta } } 1 - 4 \mathbf { \overrightarrow { \theta } }$ to ”1” for binary classification (0 indicating no heart disease and 1 for heart disease), and normalizing the dataset.

raw.criterion: <fact>Suppose we have $K$ clients, where client $k$ has $n _ { k }$ data points, and the total number of data points is $n$ , then the aggregated model at any federation round $t$ is given by $$ \theta^ {(t)} = \sum_ {k = 1} ^ {K} \frac {n _ {k}}{n} \theta_ {k} ^ {(t)} $$ where $\theta _ { k } ^ { ( t ) }$ (k are the trained model parameters at client $k$ and federation round $t$ .</fact> <scope>from paper context</scope>
raw.fact: Suppose we have $K$ clients, where client $k$ has $n _ { k }$ data points, and the total number of data points is $n$ , then the aggregated model at any federation round $t$ is given by $$ \theta^ {(t)} = \sum_ {k = 1} ^ {K} \frac {n _ {k}}{n} \theta_ {k} ^ {(t)} $$ where $\theta _ { k } ^ { ( t ) }$ (k are the trained model parameters at client $k$ and federation round $t$ .

raw.criterion: <fact>FedYogi [16] is designed to be a more adaptive and efficient variant of FedAvg, leveraging techniques to handle non-IID data distributions and optimize communication for improved federated aggregation.</fact> <scope>from paper context</scope>
raw.fact: FedYogi [16] is designed to be a more adaptive and efficient variant of FedAvg, leveraging techniques to handle non-IID data distributions and optimize communication for improved federated aggregation.

raw.criterion: <fact>Importance of features The mean absolute SHAP values for each feature across the seven tested models are presented in Table IV.</fact> <scope>from paper context</scope>
raw.fact: Importance of features The mean absolute SHAP values for each feature across the seven tested models are presented in Table IV.

raw.criterion: <fact>Ramchandran, “An efficient framework for clustered federated learning,” in the 33rd Conference on Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), 2020, pp.</fact> <scope>from paper context</scope>
raw.fact: Ramchandran, “An efficient framework for clustered federated learning,” in the 33rd Conference on Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), 2020, pp.

raw.criterion: <fact>[36] Kaggle, “Heart disease dataset,” https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/johnsmith88/heart-disease-dataset/data, n.d., accessed: February 20, 2024.</fact> <scope>dataset</scope>
raw.fact: [36] Kaggle, “Heart disease dataset,” https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/johnsmith88/heart-disease-dataset/data, n.d., accessed: February 20, 2024.

raw.criterion: <fact>Federated SVM, an uncommon approach in the literature, achieves a top testing accuracy of $7 3 .</fact> <scope>from paper context</scope>
raw.fact: Federated SVM, an uncommon approach in the literature, achieves a top testing accuracy of $7 3 .

raw.criterion: <fact>Our work enhances the benchmark for the UCI heart disease dataset included in FLamby.</fact> <scope>uci</scope>
raw.fact: Our work enhances the benchmark for the UCI heart disease dataset included in FLamby.

raw.criterion: <fact>Dataset details The dataset consists of 920 patient records spread across four separate hospitals’ databases in Cleveland, Hungary, Switzerland, and the VA Long Beach.</fact> <scope>dataset</scope>
raw.fact: Dataset details The dataset consists of 920 patient records spread across four separate hospitals’ databases in Cleveland, Hungary, Switzerland, and the VA Long Beach.
